name: Guided Tour of the Gallocanta Lagoon Natural Reserve - circular from Bello
name_original: Vuelta a la Reserva Natural Dirigida de la Laguna de Gallocanta — circular desde Bello
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Bello are situated in the Aburrá Valley, within the Andes mountain range. The urban area features a tilted plain, with elevations ranging from 1400 to 1600 meters above sea level, offering varied terrain for cyclists. Surrounding peaks, such as Cerro Quitasol, rise to 2,880 meters, contributing to the region's diverse topography. Bello is characterized by its natural features and green spaces, providing a scenic backdrop for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Bello The most popular road cycling route is Jiloca River Bank to Torralba de Sisones & Bello - circular from Calamocha, a 32.0 miles (51.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 11 minutes to complete. This moderate route features an elevation gain of over 300 meters. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Lacuna de Gallocanta - circular by the Lacuna de Gallocanta Natural Reserve, an easy 22.9 miles (36.8 km) path. This route offers a relatively flat profile with minimal elevation changes, ideal for a relaxed ride. Local road cyclists also love the Guided Tour of the Gallocanta Lagoon Natural Reserve - circular from Bello, a 22.8 miles (36.7 km) trail leading through natural reserve landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 28 minutes. Road cycling around Bello is defined by its varied elevation, natural reserves, and surrounding Andean hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Bello are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 40 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Bello's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Bello?
A: Road cycling routes around Bello offer varied terrain, from a tilted plain within the urban area (1400 to 1600 meters above sea level) to surrounding peaks like Cerro Quitasol, which rises to 2,880 meters. You'll find routes with mostly well-paved surfaces suitable for shorter rides, as well as more challenging options with significant elevation gains and potentially unpaved segments.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Bello?
A: There are 7 road cycling routes available around Bello, catering to various skill levels. This includes 2 easy routes, 4 moderate routes, and 1 difficult route, ensuring options for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or relaxed rides?
A: Yes, Bello offers easy road cycling routes. For a relaxed ride with minimal elevation changes, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963808276/lacuna-de-gallocanta-circular-by-the-lacuna-de-gallocanta-natural-reserve" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lacuna de Gallocanta - circular by the Lacuna de Gallocanta Natural Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, which is 22.9 miles (36.8 km) long. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992652458/guided-tour-of-the-gallocanta-lagoon-natural-reserve-circular-from-bello" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guided Tour of the Gallocanta Lagoon Natural Reserve - circular from Bello&lt;/a&gt;, covering 22.8 miles (36.7 km).

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Bello offers routes with significant climbs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38149466" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Daroca – Calamocha loop from Tornos&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route spanning 72.3 km with an elevation gain of nearly 700 meters. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963858964/las-tres-taifas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Tres Taifas Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 90.1 km route with over 560 meters of ascent.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling around Bello?
A: Bello is known for its majestic mountains of the Aburrá Valley and ample green spaces. Cyclists can enjoy views of prominent natural landmarks like Cerro Quitasol. Some routes also pass by significant natural reserves. You might also encounter highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2104442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallocanta Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Bello?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Bello are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968435990/jiloca-river-bank-to-torralba-de-sisones-bello-circular-from-calamocha" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jiloca River Bank to Torralba de Sisones & Bello - circular from Calamocha&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963808276/lacuna-de-gallocanta-circular-by-the-lacuna-de-gallocanta-natural-reserve" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lacuna de Gallocanta - circular by the Lacuna de Gallocanta Natural Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992652458/guided-tour-of-the-gallocanta-lagoon-natural-reserve-circular-from-bello" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guided Tour of the Gallocanta Lagoon Natural Reserve - circular from Bello&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Bello?
A: The road cycling routes in Bello are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.0 stars. Over 40 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, appreciating the diverse options from easy rides to more challenging routes with significant climbs.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Bello?
A: Bello experiences a warm climate influenced by gentle winds throughout the year, making it suitable for road cycling in most seasons. The consistent climate means you can generally plan your cycling trips without extreme weather concerns.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest near the cycling routes?
A: While cycling, you might find points of interest such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302492" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of Good Agreement&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5860483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Yunta Village and Watchtower&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the region's heritage.

Q: What are the typical elevation gains on road cycling routes in Bello?
A: Elevation gains vary significantly across routes in Bello. Easy routes like the Lacuna de Gallocanta circular have minimal changes, around 150-160 meters. Moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968435990/jiloca-river-bank-to-torralba-de-sisones-bello-circular-from-calamocha" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jiloca River Bank to Torralba de Sisones & Bello - circular from Calamocha&lt;/a&gt;, feature over 300 meters of ascent, while more challenging options can exceed 500-700 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the road cycling routes in Bello?
A: Yes, given Bello's location within the Andes mountain range and its diverse topography, many routes offer scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302514" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reguera Observatory&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is a highlight that provides excellent panoramic views, particularly over the Gallocanta Lagoon area.
tips: Gallocanta and its lagoon is one of the most important nesting sites for migratory birds in southern Europe. The cranes, for example, are a very famous species in this area where they take advantage of the waters of this protected space to live.
Small, peaceful village next to Gallocanta.
Town that is located next to the Gallocanta Lagoon and where it is characterized by having a "skyline" with a high tower that is currently a hostel, but with impressive views of the plain.
